About: It is about a royal house of Scotland, England, Ireland and later Great Britain.
Affects: It affects the succession of the British throne.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the office of High Steward of Scotland.
It is associated with the Union of the Crowns.
It is associated with the Glorious Revolution.
It is associated with the Act of Settlement 1701.
It is associated with the Act of Security 1704.
It is associated with the House of Wittelsbach.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the history of the United Kingdom.
ConsistsOf: It consists of monarchs of Scotland, England, Ireland, and Great Britain.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Walter fitz Alan.
HasA: It has a family name derived from the office of High Steward of Scotland.
It has monarchs who ruled Scotland from 1371.
It has monarchs who ruled England, Ireland and Great Britain from 1603.
It has a period of exile in mainland Europe.
It has descendants who continued to attempt to reclaim the throne.
It has a direct line that failed in the early 19th century.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the adoption of the French spelling Stuart.
It is characterized by a personal union of three kingdoms.
It is characterized by a cycle of political and military conflict.
It is characterized by the extinction of the royal house.
It is characterized by Jacobite claims to the throne.
Influenced: It influenced the succession of the British throne.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Norman Conquest.
It was influenced by The Anarchy civil war in England.
It was influenced by the House of Dunkeld.
It was influenced by Robert the Bruce.
It was influenced by the House of Tudor.
It was influenced by the Glorious Revolution.
It was influenced by the French royal family.
IsA: It is a legitimate male line.
KnownAs: It is known as the Stuart dynasty.
KnownFor: It is known for ruling Scotland, England, Ireland, and Great Britain.
It is known for the Union of the Crowns.
It is known for the period of the Commonwealth.
It is known for the Jacobite risings.
It is known for its extinction with the death of Cardinal Henry Benedict Stuart.
MemberOf: It is a member of the history of Scotland.
It is a member of the history of England.
It is a member of the history of Ireland.
It is a member of the history of Great Britain.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the 12th and 13th centuries.
It occurred during the reign of Charles I of England, Scotland and Ireland.
It occurred during the English Interregnum.
It occurred during the Commonwealth between 1649 and 1660.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Duchy of Brittany.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the office of High Steward of Scotland.
It originated from Alan fitz Flaad, a Breton.
PartOf: It is part of the history of the British monarchy.
It is part of the history of the United Kingdom.
It is part of the line of descent of the House of Tudor.
It is part of the cycle of political and military conflict.
